_readme: Golden set transcribed from Adrian's own red-pen marking (scans in ~/Desktop/AdrianMath/marking_calibration/). expected.line_verdicts aligns 1:1 with working_lines (null = don't care). expected.must_mention = case-insensitive substrings the marking JSON must contain somewhere (correction/summary). Grow this set as more scripts are transcribed — especially items where the current prompt disagrees with Adrian.

items.question: G is a point outside circle with centre O. GA and GB are tangents to the circle at A and B. Explain why a circle can be drawn passing through A, O, B and G, stating the diameter.
working_lines: The centre will be midpoint of GO
items.note: Adrian accepted the midpoint claim but supplied the missing justification chain in red: ∠GAO = ∠GBO = 90° (tan ⊥ rad), right angles in semicircle, AB... circle through A,O,B,G with GO as diameter. A bare assertion without the tangent-radius reason is an incomplete explanation.

items.question: In the circle with centre O, ∠AOB = 126°. P, C are points on the circumference. Find ∠OAB and hence ∠DCA, giving reasons.
items.note: Fully correct with reasons stated — Adrian ticks every line, no deduction, no commentary.

items.question: In the diagram, K is the midpoint of QR, H is the midpoint of PH and HR... Given PR = 2PH and QH = HS, prove that PQ = SR.
items.note: Adrian: 'does not prove PQ = SR' — restating givens then asserting the conclusion is a logical gap worth all the marks; his red pen supplies the midpoint-theorem chain (PQ = 2HK and SR = 2HK ∴ PQ = SR).

items.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. APQ, BRQ, CRS and DPS are straight lines which bisect angles A, B, C and D respectively. Show that triangle ADP and triangle CBR are congruent. Give a reason for each statement you make.
items.note: Adrian marked −2 of [3]: first statement fine (he tightened the reason to 'opp sides of parallelogram'), second is wrong (not vertically opposite), and the attempt never reaches a congruency test. His red pen writes the complete model proof ending '∴ △ADP ≡ △CBR (ASA)'.

items.question: Line AB: y = 2x + h. Line BC: x + 2y = 12h. B is the intersection of AB and BC. Find the coordinates of B in terms of h.
items.note: The student mis-copied 12h where rearranging gives 6h. Adrian circles the wrong token, writes 6 above, then ECF: the subsequent solving METHOD is followed through as correct (his red continuation shows x=2h, y=5h, B(2h,5h)). One error, one deduction — later lines are not re-penalized.

items.question: In the circle, reflex ∠AOB is required. The minor angle relationships are given via the tangent at A. Find ∠AOB in radians, where the tangent-chord angle at A is π/4.
working_lines: reflex ∠AOB = (π/4) × 2 = π/2 (∠ at centre is 2 times ∠ at circumference)
items.note: Adrian: underlines the quoted property, ✗, 'this property is not relevant in this question', and 'discuss in class and try again' — a conceptual misapplication rather than a slip, so the summary should defer to class discussion.
